title: Empty workspace — no source code delivered to the run directory
evidence: find . -type f lists only TASK.md, stack.json, _meta.json, scores.json, and logs (7 files, 0 source). No src/, package.json, README.md, or tests/ exist. Agent stdout says it created the project at absolute path '/Users/adriancockcroft/book-api/' — outside the sandboxed workspace.
suggestion: Agent must write deliverables into the workspace CWD, not an absolute path under $HOME. This run cannot be scored on its own artifacts.

title: Agent wrote files outside its workspace, polluting the user home directory
evidence: _agent_stdout.log: 'Created a complete TypeScript project ... in /Users/adriancockcroft/book-api/'. That directory now contains the TS project plus stale Python files (app.py, models.py, test_app.py) from a prior run and a literal file named ':memory:?cache=shared' — cross-run pollution of a shared out-of-sandbox path.
suggestion: Confine agent file writes to the run workspace; reject absolute paths under $HOME. Clean up /Users/adriancockcroft/book-api/.

title: scores.json does not reflect the archived workspace
evidence: scores.json reports test_coverage=0.6597, code_quality=0.7333, maintainability=0.6964 — impossible for a workspace with zero source files. The scorer evaluated the leaked external directory (or CWD), not this empty archive.
suggestion: Treat this run as failed regardless of stored scores; the scored artifacts are not in the archive. Investigate the scorer's CWD handling for out-of-workspace agents.

title: Agent self-reports integration tests failing
evidence: _agent_stdout.log: 'The integration tests are failing due to a database initialization issue with SQLite in-memory database ... only the test database initialization needs to be fixed.' Completed=false in .hermes_usage.json.
suggestion: Use a file-based or properly shared SQLite connection in tests; the run left tests red.
